# Security & Data Protection Policy (SIA)

This document defines core security practices, focusing on secrets management, prompt injection defenses, and safe autonomous code execution.

## 1. Secrets Management
* **Zero Hardcoded Secrets**: Absolutely no API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub, etc.) in Git.
* Use environment variables (`.env`) or secure environment injection during runtime.

## 2. Safe Code Execution & Sandboxing
* **Runtime Isolation**: Code generated by SIA must be executed within isolated sandbox environments (e.g., Docker, VM, or restricted Node VM contexts) when evaluating system changes.
* **No Arbitrary Commands**: The agent loop must not execute raw shell commands directly on the host machine without a pre-approved whitelist of safe commands or human approval.

## 3. Prompt Injection Protection
* Any external data fetched by the agent (e.g., scraping web pages, reading third-party files) must be treated as untrusted input.
* Sanitize all external inputs before injecting them into agent prompt templates.

## 4. GitHub and Repository Access
* Limit GitHub access tokens used by the agent to repository-specific scopes with minimal write privileges.
* Do not allow the agent to force-push directly to `main` without human confirmation.
